vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high avionics heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Implement additional heat sinks and thermal management systems.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient load distribution from wing spar to fuselage spine.
risk.mit: Conduct FEA analysis to validate load paths and reinforce if necessary.

risk.desc: Electrical routing complexity may lead to installation errors.
risk.mit: Develop detailed installation guides and conduct training sessions.

physics_paths.description: Wing spar loads into fuselage spine
Avionics heat dissipation through fuselage shell

Electrical harness routing from avionics bay to external ports

Mount GPS antenna on front boom

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion mismatch between CFRP and aluminum components.
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion coefficients to design interfaces with appropriate clearance.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient preload on bolted interfaces due to improper torque application.
risk.mit: Include torque specifications and use calibrated tools during assembly.

risk.desc: Possibility of water ingress through service access panels.
risk.mit: Design with proper sealing and drainage features.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if cooling channels are not properly aligned with heat sources.
risk.mit: Ensure precise alignment and use thermal analysis to validate design.

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment during assembly due to complex interface geometries.
risk.mit: Implement alignment aids and verify fit during prototype assembly.

align bearing seats before clamping to wing

verification_focus.description: Check tilt axis rotation range and torque transmission efficiency

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure

physics_paths.description: Transfers rotor thrust and tilt servo loads from nacelle to fuselage spine via wing spar and spar_socket.
Power and control signals from servo module to ruddervator via harness_passthrough.
Heat dissipation from environment to TPU gasket via natural convection.
Servo actuation of ruddervator via piano wire hinge.
Field technician accesses servo module and piano wire through access cut.

integration_opportunities.description: Bolt pattern for wing spar attachment.
Hinge pocket for piano wire attachment.
Bolt pattern for fuselage spine attachment.

verify.rule: Check if all required fields and features are present in the subsystem plan.
Ensure the mass estimate is a numeric value and realistic for the subsystem.

Each interface must have a reciprocal joint family named in the neighbor subsystem.

risk.desc: Potential material fatigue in spring-steel skid arms due to repeated landing impacts.
risk.mit: Conduct fatigue tests and consider material upgrades.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance in critical interfaces leading to jamming or seizing.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearances in moving parts to ensure smooth operation.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion causing misalignment in electrical connections.
risk.mit: Use thermally stable materials and consider thermal management solutions.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access for maintenance and inspection.
risk.mit: Ensure all critical components have easy access for servicing.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to overloading or improper load distribution.
risk.mit: Conduct load tests and structural analysis to ensure load paths are properly designed.

generated_parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay

verify.rule: All required fields are present in the output.
The mass estimate is within the expected range for the subsystem.
All P0 internal_feature_targets are represented in the geometry_ops.
Each interface has a reciprocal connection_type and DOF defined.
Critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.desc: The harness routing through the tilt axis may be susceptible to mechanical stress during rotation.
risk.mit: Implement strain relief features at the exit points of the harness.

risk.desc: The service hatch may not provide sufficient clearance for all tools and components.
risk.mit: Review and adjust the clearance dimensions to accommodate the required tools and components.

risk.desc: The tilt axis bearing may experience wear over time due to repeated rotation.
risk.mit: Select a high-quality bearing with a longer lifespan and conduct regular maintenance checks.

risk.desc: The service window may not provide adequate access for maintenance and inspection.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the service window or add additional access points.

risk.desc: The interface datum may not provide sufficient alignment accuracy.
risk.mit: Improve the datum design to ensure higher alignment accuracy.
